# DELTA DIY MRI Travel Award Recipient Introduction

Please replace the bracketed text below and return the completed Markdown file to the organizers.

---

## Saar Drive

**Affiliation:** Technion - Israel Institute of Technology 
**Current role:** Graduate student
**Location:** [Haifa, Israel]  
**Workshop interests:** Data acquisition | Reconstruction

I am a Master's student in Electrical Engineering at the Technion, under the supervision of Dr. Efrat Shimron. My work focuses on reconstructing undersampled MRI data using advanced deep learning architectures. In parallel, I program custom pulse sequences using the open-source Pulseq framework to prospectively acquire novel data. I am passionate about leveraging deep learning to optimize data acquisition, hoping to contribute to the shared mission of accessible MRI. At the workshop, I hope to explore open-source integrations and exchange insights on advanced reconstruction techniques. Afterward, I plan to integrate these DIY hardware concepts with our lab’s ongoing research.

**Find me online:** LinkedIn:https://www.linkedin.com/in/saar-drive/ | GitHub: https://github.com/saardrive
